unlock.title: Unlock identity
unlock.help: Your passphrase decrypts the identity that guards your secrets. It lives only on this device — gpm cannot recover or reset it for you. If you lose it, your secrets are permanently lost; you would need to reset gpm (Settings → Danger Zone) and set it up again.
unlock.passphraseHint: Enter the passphrase to unlock your identity
unlock.biometricResetNotice: Biometric was reset (new fingerprint?) — re-enable it in Settings.
unlock.biometricStaleNotice: Stored passphrase is invalid — re-enable biometric in Settings.
unlock.lockModeImmediate: Identity is cleared after every action.
unlock.lockModeNever: Identity stays unlocked until you lock manually.
unlock.lockModeIdle: Identity auto-locks after {mins} min of inactivity.

appLock.title: App locked
appLock.keyInvalidatedNotice: All fingerprints were removed, so gpm can no longer unlock your app key. Clear gpm's app data from Android Settings (or uninstall and reinstall) to set it up again.

passphraseAck.warning: Your passphrase cannot be recovered. gpm has no reset — if you lose it, your secrets are permanently lost. Store it somewhere safe (e.g. a password manager) before continuing.
passphraseAck.ack: I understand this passphrase cannot be recovered, and losing it will permanently lock me out of my secrets.

divergence.headingSave: Your edit conflicts with a newer remote
divergence.saveHint: Adopting discards your local edit; keeping it may overwrite the remote entries below.
divergence.syncHint: Adopting discards the local-only changes below; keeping pushes them (and may overwrite the remote entries).
divergence.confirmAdoptFooter: The remote versions will be kept.
divergence.confirmPushLine1: Your local changes will be pushed. This may overwrite the remote version of:
divergence.confirmPushNoShared: No shared entries differ — only your local-only entries are pushed.

authenticity.heading: Sync blocked
authenticity.body: Enforce mode refused to update the store — HEAD did not advance. Resolve the signature issue, then sync again.
authenticity.gpgNotice: GPG-signed commit {hash} was made by a signer you haven't trusted. GPG signatures don't embed the public key, so open {link} and add (or import) that signer's armored public key to verify it.
